title: PV Sindhu Conquers Japan Open, Ends Two-Year Title Drought
indian_angle: PV Sindhu became the first Indian to win the Japan Open, marking a significant return to form after a challenging two years.
summary: India's badminton star PV Sindhu defeated Akane Yamaguchi in the Japan Open final, securing her first BWF title in two years and becoming the first Indian to win this prestigious event.

title: Inside PV Sindhu's Data-Driven Comeback to Japan Open Glory
indian_angle: This feature details the radical, data-driven approach by sports scientist Wayne Lombard that helped Indian stalwart PV Sindhu regain her top form.
summary: Sports scientist Wayne Lombard sheds light on the meticulous, data-driven training regimen that propelled PV Sindhu to her recent Japan Open victory, marking a significant turnaround in her career.

title: BAI Announces Eligibility and Selection Tournament for World Junior Championships 2026
indian_angle: The Badminton Association of India has released crucial guidelines for player eligibility and confirmed the selection tournament for Indian participants at the upcoming World Junior Championships in Cairo.
summary: The BAI has issued a circular detailing the eligibility criteria for Indian players aiming for the BWF World Junior Championships 2026 in Cairo, also designating the Bhubaneswar U-19 Ranking Tournament as a key selection event.

title: 3x15 Scoring System Introduced for Indian Junior Domestic Tournaments
indian_angle: This new scoring format will be implemented across all domestic ranking tournaments and national championships for U-13, U-15, and U-17 age groups in India.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has announced the implementation of a 3x15 scoring system for all domestic ranking tournaments and national championships in the U-13, U-15, and U-17 age categories, starting soon.

title: Yonex-Sunrise All India Senior Ranking Tournament Underway in Chennai
indian_angle: This major domestic tournament in Chennai provides a crucial platform for India's senior shuttlers to compete and improve their national rankings.
summary: The Yonex-Sunrise All India Senior Ranking Badminton Tournament 2026 is currently being held in Chennai, Tamil Nadu, from July 16th to 23rd, featuring top Indian senior players.
